node.title: Uber | Phone | Minimum numbers to cover all intervals

topic.title: Uber | Phone | Minimum numbers to cover all intervals

post.content:
Given a list of tuples consisting of interval start and end time (both inclusive), find a set of numbers with minimum size such that at least k numbers from each interval are covered. Start by choosing k = 2. You can assume each interval will have size at least k.

Examples for k = 2: 
[[1,4], [5,6]] - [1,4,5,6]
[[1,4], [3,6], [2,4]] - [3,4]
[[1,2], [2,3], [2,5]] - [1,2,3]
[[1,10], [3,7], [5,6]] - [5,6]
[[1,5], [4,7], [6,8], [9,13]] - [4,5,6,7,9,13]
[[1,10], [5,7], [7,13]] - [6,7,8]

I used a modification of interval intersection to identify common elements and propogate it through the sorted list. Was able to generate a linear solution for k=2 (if the list is already sorted, otherwise nlogn). Did not have sufficient time for generic k. Interviewer was surprised that my clumsy code worked for all test cases that he had. I guess that acted as a savior to not get rejected and have been called for another phone screen.
Python greedy solution:\n\n<iframe src="https://leetcode.com/playground/LAYPj4fb/shared" frameBorder="0" width="80%" height="300"></iframe>

this is very similar to https://leetcode.com/problems/minimum-number-of-arrows-to-burst-balloons/ (there one picks 1 element, here pick k elements per interval), one need to sort on increasing end time, so if time interval is covered then continue; else pick last k elements, if n elements (n< k) picked for this interval take the last k-n elements.
